name: Pune
description: Pune is a magnificent destination in Maharashtra, India. Known for its remarkable blend of history, culture, and natural beauty. Visitors flock here to experience its unique atmosphere, vibrant local markets, and iconic landmarks. Whether you are seeking spiritual peace, thrilling adventures, or simply a relaxing holiday, Pune offers a memorable experience that captures the true essence of Indian heritage. The city's charm lies in its timeless appeal and warm hospitality.
history: Pune boasts a rich history spanning several centuries, serving as a prominent center for trade, culture, and power under various dynasties.

topAttractions.name: Aga Khan Palace
topAttractions.description: Built in 1892, this majestic palace served as a prison for Mahatma Gandhi and his wife Kasturba Gandhi during the 1942 Quit India Movement. Kasturba died here, and her samadhi is in the palace grounds. It's now a museum of Gandhi's life. Entry: ₹25.

topAttractions.name: Shaniwar Wada
topAttractions.description: The historic 1732 fortification that served as the seat of the Peshwas of the Maratha Empire until 1818. Largely destroyed by an unexplained fire in 1828, the imposing walls, gateways (Dilli Darwaza), and foundations remain. Evening sound and light show. Entry: ₹25.

topAttractions.name: Dagadusheth Halwai Ganapati Temple
topAttractions.description: One of the most famous Hindu temples in Maharashtra, dedicated to Lord Ganesha. The idol is adorned with nearly 40 kilos of gold. During the 10-day Ganeshotsav festival, millions of devotees visit. It remains open from 6 AM to 11 PM.

topAttractions.name: Osho International Meditation Resort
topAttractions.description: A sprawling, lush ashram in Koregaon Park founded by the controversial mystic Rajneesh (Osho). Attracts thousands of international visitors for its meditation programs. The environment is serene with black marble paths and greenery. Entry is restricted (day passes available for ₹1000-2000).

topAttractions.name: Sinhagad Fort
topAttractions.description: A massive 17th-century hill fort located 30km from Pune at an elevation of 1312m. Famous for the 1670 Battle of Sinhagad fought by Tanaji Malusare. A popular trekking destination offering spectacular views of the Sahyadri mountains and Khadakwasla Dam. The hike takes about 1-2 hours from the base.

topAttractions.name: Raja Dinkar Kelkar Museum
topAttractions.description: A fascinating one-man collection by Dr. Dinkar Kelkar, housing over 20,000 artifacts from 18th and 19th-century India. Includes musical instruments, hookahs, writing instruments, and a replica of the Mastani Mahal. Entry: ₹100.

culture: The culture of Pune is a vibrant tapestry of traditional arts, festivals, and culinary delights, deeply influenced by its historical roots.
travelTips: Book accommodations in advance during peak season
Try the local street food but ensure it is hot and fresh
Use authorized taxis or rideshare apps
Respect local customs and dress modestly at religious sites

itineraries.name: Highlights Tour
activities: Day 1: Arrival, City Center Attractions, Evening Market
Day 2: Historical Sites, Local Food Tasting, Departure
